title: DirectiveArchetype
description: Schema for Directive Ascriptions — identity-level normative constraints (DNA 'D'). A Directive is a constitutive act that opens a governance relationship between a governing Structure (structure) and a governed Structure (purpose) along a viability dimension (qualifier Archetype). Without a Directive, there is no governance scope — no Norms can constrain the governed Structure's internal Elements along that dimension. Natural grammar: {structure} {modal} {verb} {qualifier} FOR {purpose}. Extensible — tenant-defined archetypes MAY extend Directive via allOf to add domain-specific vocabulary while inheriting the governance grammar. DM input = DM output (1:1).

structure.description: ID reference to the governing Structure that issues the Directive — the Structure accountable for this governance commitment. Scope rule: the governance scope opened by this Directive covers the internal Elements of the governed Structure (purpose) — its Mechanisms, Effectors, Receptors, and Interactions. Scope is NOT accumulated across Directives.

modal.description: Deontic obligation strength. MUST/MUST_NOT = hard constraint (violations = errors). SHOULD/SHOULD_NOT = soft constraint (violations = warnings). MAY = permitted (informational).

verb.description: Governance action expressing the governance direction on the qualifier Archetype's viability dimension.

qualifier.description: ID reference to the Archetype that defines the viability dimension being governed. Norms operationalizing this Directive MUST reference this same Archetype (or a descendant) in their applicability/assertion CEL expressions.

purpose.description: ID reference to the purposed Structure whose purpose is governed by this Directive.
